text: fat is 42g. saturated fat is a major pressure point. for meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.
carbs is 1.4g. everything else is lackluster. in this category, carbs barely matter.
protein is 37g. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.
vitamin b12 at 20% DV, vitamin d at 2% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
minerals are basically not part of the case here.

pros first. highly palatable and appealing because a real-world reason people love it. works as a strong flavour booster because useful in meals even in smaller amounts. has strong comfort-food appeal because popular and instantly legible for content packaging.

the drawbacks next. sodium is a major drawback because a central practical health issue for bacon. processing is a major negative because a key downside now that more processed foods are in scope. rarely improves a meal much on its own because usually works more as a flavour add-on than a genuinely strong meat choice.

highly palatable and appealing and works as a strong flavour booster help bacon. but sodium is a major drawback and processing is a major negative drag it down hard. best only if convenience, budget, or taste matters more than getting the cleanest meat option. D tier.
